Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Dumping Lego NXT firmware off of an existing brick (2025) 220 upvotes, 13 comments
  2. CasNum 302 upvotes, 36 comments
  3. Emacs internals: Deconstructing Lisp_Object in C (Part 2) 87 upvotes, 8 comments
  4. The Collective Ambition Behind Odysseus, a Game-Changing Sci-Fi Larp 27 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. In 1985 Maxell built a bunch of life-size robots for its bad floppy ad 120 upvotes, 17 comments
  6. Sem – Semantic version control. Entity-level diffs on top of Git 34 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. Show HN: Curiosity – DIY 6" Newtonian Reflector Telescope 43 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Some Words on WigglyPaint 27 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 55 upvotes, 10 comments
  10. Autoresearch: Agents researching on single-GPU nanochat training automatically 135 upvotes, 33 comments
  11. How to run Qwen 3.5 locally 235 upvotes, 66 comments
  12. SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI 69 upvotes, 15 comments
  13. Digital Iris [video] 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. FLASH radiotherapy's bold approach to cancer treatment 215 upvotes, 65 comments
  15. The surprising whimsy of the Time Zone Database 140 upvotes, 46 comments
  16. Show HN: Eyot, A programming language where the GPU is just another thread 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. Apple's 512GB Mac Studio vanishes, a quiet acknowledgment of the RAM shortage 76 upvotes, 25 comments
  18. I ported Linux to the PS5 and turned it into a Steam Machine 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. From RGB to L*a*b* color space (2024) 55 upvotes, 18 comments
  20. Cloud VM benchmarks 2026 274 upvotes, 123 comments
  21. The Lobster Programming Language 33 upvotes, 9 comments
  22. Warn about PyPy being unmaintained 231 upvotes, 107 comments
  23. Yoghurt delivery women combatting loneliness in Japan 320 upvotes, 168 comments
  24. Files are the interface humans and agents interact with 242 upvotes, 124 comments
  25. Show HN: A weird thing that detects your pulse from the browser video 94 upvotes, 43 comments
  26. Notes on Writing WASM 40 upvotes, 18 comments
  27. A decade of Docker containers 318 upvotes, 216 comments
  28. MonoGame: A .NET framework for making cross-platform games 90 upvotes, 59 comments
  29. Best performance of a C++ singleton 37 upvotes, 34 comments
  30. Ask HN: How to Be Alone? 9 upvotes, 10 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 8 March 2026. Source here.
